getting nowhere.explanation_en: making no progress or having no success
getting nowhere.example_sentence: I have been trying to fix this computer for hours, but I am getting nowhere.
getting nowhere.original_sentence: He was employed as a clerk in the credit department of a mail order company and felt that he was getting nowhere.

move up the ladder.explanation_en: to advance in one's career or social status
move up the ladder.example_sentence: She worked hard for years to move up the ladder and become a manager.
move up the ladder.original_sentence: After knowing something about him, I said, I admire you very much for wanting to move up the ladder to a better job and more responsibility.

land.explanation_en: to succeed in getting something, especially a job
land.example_sentence: He was so happy when he finally landed a job at the bank.
land.original_sentence: Then I'm sure you can land the job you want, with the company you want to work for.

on top of that.explanation_en: in addition to what has already been mentioned
on top of that.example_sentence: The hotel was expensive, and on top of that, it was very far from the city center.
on top of that.original_sentence: On top of that, my wife and I are expecting our second child in a couple of months.

get by.explanation_en: to have just enough money to pay for the things you need
get by.example_sentence: It is difficult to get by on such a low salary in this city.
get by.original_sentence: We barely get by now on what I make.

by the same token.explanation_en: used to introduce a statement that is true for the same reasons as the previous one
by the same token.example_sentence: I don't like horror movies; by the same token, I don't like scary books.
by the same token.original_sentence: But by the same token, if you'll just believe it is possible to return to the university, a solution will come.

make up your mind.example_sentence: You need to make up your mind about which university to attend.
make up your mind.original_sentence: Make up your mind you are going to go back to school.

dominate.explanation_en: to be the most important or noticeable part of something
dominate.example_sentence: The fear of failure dominated his thoughts.
dominate.original_sentence: Let that one thought dominate your thinking.

come up with.explanation_en: to suggest or think of an idea or plan
come up with.example_sentence: She came up with a great idea for the party.
come up with.original_sentence: Come back in a couple of weeks and let me know what ideas you've come up with.

figure out.explanation_en: to understand or solve a problem
figure out.example_sentence: I can't figure out how to use this new software.
figure out.original_sentence: I haven't figured out all the angles yet, but I'll find a solution.

scholarship.explanation_en: an amount of money given by a school or organization to help a student pay for their studies
scholarship.example_sentence: He won a scholarship to study at a top university.
scholarship.original_sentence: He managed to get a scholarship provided by a trade association, which paid his tuition, books, and incidentals.

tuition.explanation_en: the money you pay to be taught at a college or university
tuition.example_sentence: The cost of tuition has increased this year.
tuition.original_sentence: He managed to get a scholarship provided by a trade association, which paid his tuition, books, and incidentals.

rearrange.explanation_en: to change the order or time of something
rearrange.example_sentence: I need to rearrange my schedule to fit in a doctor's appointment.
rearrange.original_sentence: He rearranged his work schedule so he could attend classes.

enthusiasm.explanation_en: a feeling of energetic interest in a particular subject or activity
enthusiasm.example_sentence: She showed great enthusiasm for the new project.
enthusiasm.original_sentence: His enthusiasm and the promise of a better life won him his wife's full support.

where there's a will, there is a way.explanation_en: if you are determined enough, you can find a way to achieve what you want
where there's a will, there is a way.example_sentence: It was hard to learn the language, but where there's a will, there is a way.
where there's a will, there is a way.original_sentence: Where there's a will, there is a way.
